items.title: Federal Reserve issues FOMC statement
items.description: Federal Reserve issues FOMC statement

items.title: Bitcoin stalls as open interest decline raises questions about rally's staying power
items.description: BTC retreated from a two-week high of $64,500 as falling open interest and weak spot demand cast doubt on the sustainability of July's 8.4% advance.

items.title: Binance taps into Bitcoin holders’ hunger for yield with new covered call yield play
items.description: The product, called BTC Yield, is designed exclusively for people who already hold bitcoin.

items.title: Bitcoin's recent macro relief faces a challenge from Japanese interest rates
items.description: Japanese bond yields continue to rise, lifting their U.S. counterparts and posing a potential headwind for risk assets.

items.title: Live markets: Bitcoin and ether ETFs drew inflows on Monday
items.description: Spot bitcoin ETFs still lost a net $526.6 million over the shortened holiday week, an eighth straight week of negative flows.

items.title: BONK faces $20 million treasury drain after attacker spends $4 million to pass malicious proposal
items.description: The attacker bought enough of the memecoin's tokens to pass a governance proposal that sent the group's holdings to a wallet they controlled, then began selling.

items.title: XRP stalls near $1.14 as breakout attempt struggles for volume
items.description: Buyers defended the session lows and pushed XRP back toward resistance, but muted overall volume left traders waiting for confirmation above $1.13-$1.14.

items.title: Bitcoin drops after a run at $64,000, shrugging off Strategy's $213 million BTC sale
items.description: The token touched $64,400 overnight before easing back, still up about 6% on the week. A fresh missile strike on a Qatari gas ship in the Strait of Hormuz lifted oil and tested the late-June peace deal as Asian tech sold off again.

items.title: Bitcoin's U.S. reserve still a work-in-progress as federal agencies hash it out
items.description: The White House says it's still evaluating the "best structure" for the federal fund to hold bitcoin as a long-term reserve and a separate stockpile of other crypto assets.

items.title: Ctrl Wallet to shut weeks after security exploit
items.description: The move follows a June 23 exploit that has now resulted in closing down the wallet, with users urged to withdraw their assets before all functions are disabled on Aug. 3, 2026.

items.title: Vitalik Buterin confirms AI identified his anonymous Ethereum proposal contribution
items.description: The Ethereum co-founder said AI recognized his intellectual habits, ending a two-week public challenge to identify an anonymous contribution he made.

items.title: Bitcoin can fall below $58K if one of its 'cleanest' metrics copies history: Analysis
items.description: Bitcoin NUPL metric data suggested that BTC price action should make new cycle lows in order to preserve historical patterns.

items.title: Digital Chamber amicus brief urges dismissal of NY lawsuit over 39,069 Bitcoin wallets
items.description: The Digital Chamber filed an amicus brief urging the dismissal of the New York lawsuit seeking ownership of 39,069 dormant Bitcoin wallets, arguing it would set a dangerous precedent for self-custodial wallets.

items.title: MiCA-compliant euro stablecoins grew 128% before MiCA transition ended, says Decta
items.description: Decta's report said the market cap of eight MiCA-compliant euro stablecoins rose to $673.9 million in the year before Europe’s CASP transition period ended.

items.title: Here’s what happened in crypto today
items.description: Need to know what happened in crypto today? Here is the latest news on daily trends and events impacting Bitcoin price, blockchain, DeFi, Web3 and crypto regulation.

items.title: Trader loses $2M in ‘same-block backrun extraction’ exploit
items.description: One crypto trader noted the $2 million loss could have been prevented had the victim read the transaction route before signing the transaction.

items.title: Yield Guild Games cuts 35 staff, shuts game publisher to focus on AI
items.description: Yield Guild Games said tough crypto and video game market conditions mean its YGG Play business is no longer commercially sustainable.

items.title: Oil prices rise after report of Iranian attack on commercial ships in Strait of Hormuz
items.description: Brent and U.S. crude futures rose after a report of an Iranian attack on commercial ships in the Strait of Hormuz.

items.title: Trump arrives in Turkey as NATO is strained by Russian attacks, U.S. impatience
items.description: Trump frequently vented about NATO members' refusal to heed U.S. calls for help clearing the Strait of Hormuz during its campaign against Iran.

items.title: Ukrainian drones hit Russia’s largest oil refinery as Zelenskyy says Siberia now ‘within reach’
items.description: The attack underscores Kyiv's enhanced long-range drone capabilities and comes on the eve of a crucial NATO summit.

items.title: Far more real estate agents now report seeing a balanced market, CNBC Housing Market Survey finds
items.description: Agents who reported at least one price cut to active listings dropped dramatically from prior surveys.

items.title: Samsung posts 1,800% jump in profit, but AI spending concerns spook investors
items.description: Samsung Electronics shares fell even after record preliminary second-quarter profit as capex and demand concerns clouded the outlook.

items.title: Toyota to invest $3.6 billion to move Tacoma pickup truck production from Mexico to Texas
items.description: Toyota said it is investing $3.6 billion to move production of the Tacoma midsize pickup truck from Mexico to its San Antonio manufacturing campus in Texas.

items.title: Graham Platner denies sex assault claim as Democrats urge him to quit Maine Senate race
items.description: Graham Platner hopes to unseat Republican Sen. Susan Collins, but the Democrat's campaign is reeling from a bombshell allegation by Jenny Racicot.

items.title: Billionaire John Arnold commits $2.6 million to study online sports betting risk
items.description: John Arnold, who runs Arnold Ventures with his wife, Laura, is investing $2.6 million for university research into potential harms of online sports betting.

items.title: Bitcoin hovers above $63k as traders weigh fresh Iran tensions, tech selloff
Bitcoin tops $64k as Trump’s ’big crypto guy’ comment offsets Strategy sale

items.transcript_reason:
Could not retrieve a transcript for the video https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=aPOzOf44LEc! This is most likely caused by:

YouTube is blocking requests from your IP. This usually is due to one of the following reasons:
- You have done too many requests and your IP has been blocked by YouTube
- You are doing requests from an IP belonging to a cloud provider (like AWS, Google Cloud Platform, Azure, etc.). Unfortunately, most IPs from cloud providers are blocked by YouTube.

There are two things you can do to work around this:
1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: SpaceX’s two lead underwriters have a $1 trillion chasm in their valuation as quiet period ends
items.description: The two lead underwriters on SpaceX’s initial public offering, Goldman Sachs and Morgan Stanley, have a valuation gap of more than $1 trillion as they both initiated coverage at buy.

items.title: The bull market got a new lease on life — can it last?
items.description: The market-timers’ bullishness is a mile wide but only an inch deep

items.title: ‘Our family is broken beyond repair’: My brother took over my parents’ finances. What can I do?
items.description: “Without telling me, my brother petitioned for sole guardianship of our mother.”

items.title: Why these global stocks with a high barrier to entry are winning the war on AI disruption
items.description: Goldman Sachs says the HALO trade has worked well this year, but a tougher phase relying on earnings performance lies ahead.

items.title: Just how K-shaped is the U.S. economy? The top 10% spend nearly as much, excluding essentials, as bottom 70% combined
items.description: The gulf between the rich and everyone else is laid out in a stark analysis done by Bank of America’s economists on household spending.

items.title: ‘I make my own pizza.’ What’s the best way you’ve found to save money?
items.description: “Saving money doesn’t have to be about pizza — it could be coffee, cooking, cleaning, vacations, or anything else.”

items.title: ‘The money would have been life-changing’: My father told my grandmother to slash my inheritance. Is that fair?
items.description: “I could have bought a home for what I hope will be a growing family.”

items.title: Tough crowd for Samsung as record quarterly earnings brings out the sellers
items.description: Samsung Electronics shareholders proved a difficult audience to please.

1. Use proxies to hide your IP address, as explained in the "Working around IP bans" section of the README (https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api?tab=readme-ov-file#working-around-ip-bans-requestblocked-or-ipblocked-exception).
2. (NOT RECOMMENDED) If you authenticate your requests using cookies, you will be able to continue doing requests for a while. However, YouTube will eventually permanently ban the account that you have used to authenticate with! So only do this if you don't mind your account being banned!

If you are sure that the described cause is not responsible for this error and that a transcript should be retrievable, please create an issue at https://github.com/jdepoix/youtube-transcript-api/issues. Please add which version of youtube_transcript_api you are using and provide the information needed to replicate the error. Also make sure that there are no open issues which already describe your problem!

items.title: Energy transition or energy addition? What the latest data shows
items.description: Ten takeaways from the Energy Institute’s 75th annual Statistical Review of World Energy

items.title: Explosions shake Damascus as Macron visits
items.description: French president is first EU leader to travel to Syria since fall of Assad regime

items.title: Bank of England plans to ease bank capital rule
items.description: Officials say move is intended to boost lending and support financial markets in a crisis

items.title: Has the Iran war changed the world order? You asked, Gideon Rachman answered
items.description: The FT’s chief foreign affairs commentator replied to reader questions in an Ask an Expert Q&A

items.title: FirstFT: How Europe would fight without America
items.description: Also in today’s newsletter: HSBC’s private credit exposure and Platner takes ‘time to reflect’

items.title: Burnham warned of ‘unsustainable’ public finances by UK fiscal watchdog
items.description: Office for Budget Responsibility’s annual report says urgent action is needed to cut spending

items.title: Japanese wages continue to rise, boosting BoJ normalisation policy
items.description: Uptick in wages alongside inflation is what central bank wants to see to persist with lifting interest rates

items.title: SpaceX wins bullish recommendations from Wall Street banks
items.description: Morgan Stanley gives Musk’s AI and rocket stock $300 price target as quiet period ends following record IPO

items.title: Those Soldiers Flooding Your Feeds? They Might Not Be Real.
items.description: Videos generated by artificial intelligence have swarmed social media to exploit public support of U.S. troops for other purposes.

items.title: Why Are Berries Everywhere, in Every Season? Driscoll’s.
items.description: The California giant has helped turn a local, seasonal treat into a worldwide refrigerator staple and marketing juggernaut.

items.title: Four Seasons Sets Sail, Offering Luxury and Curated Cruise Experiences
items.description: Luxury hotel brands are getting into the cruise business. A Mediterranean itinerary offered a taste of what at times seemed like a mega-yacht experience.

items.title: As Messi Dominates the World Cup, a War Is Waged Over Maradona’s Image
items.description: A multimillion-dollar battle for a soccer god’s brand is being waged across continents, burning investors and tearing his family apart.

items.title: Iranian Strikes on Ships Test Cease-Fire and Threaten Energy Flows in Gulf
items.description: A U.S. official said Iranian missiles struck two commercial ships. There was no immediate comment from Iranian officials on the reported attacks.

items.title: Samsung Made More Profit Last Quarter Than the Last Two Years Combined
items.description: The A.I. boom has propelled the South Korean tech giant’s earnings, but its shares sank as investors had even loftier expectations.

items.title: George E. Johnson, Who Built a Black Hair-Care Empire, Dies at 99
items.description: Mr. Johnson’s hair product company, a longtime sponsor of “Soul Train,” was the first Black-owned company listed on a major American stock exchange.

items.title: Richard H. Glanton, Combative Head of Barnes Foundation, Dies at 79
items.description: In the 1990s, he took 80 of the art institution’s paintings on tour — going against the founder’s wishes — to raise money for a renovation.
